verses.text: Balaam, seeing God's pleasure, does not seek enchantments. The Spirit. God's Spirit comes upon him.
Balaam, seeing God's pleasure, does not seek enchantments. The Spirit. God's Spirit comes upon him.
Balaam, seeing God's pleasure, does not seek enchantments. The Spirit. God's Spirit comes upon him.
He sees Israel's goodly tents spread like gardens. The vision. He beholds their beauty.
He sees Israel's goodly tents spread like gardens. The vision. He beholds their beauty.
He sees Israel's goodly tents spread like gardens. The vision. He beholds their beauty.
'Blessed is he that blesseth thee, and cursed is he that curseth thee.' Blessed. He echoes the promise to Abraham.
'Blessed is he that blesseth thee, and cursed is he that curseth thee.' Blessed. He echoes the promise to Abraham.
'Blessed is he that blesseth thee, and cursed is he that curseth thee.' Blessed. He echoes the promise to Abraham.
Balak is furious and sends Balaam away. Dismissed. The hireling earns no reward.
Balak is furious and sends Balaam away. Dismissed. The hireling earns no reward.
Balaam says he warned he could speak only God's word. Forewarned. He kept God's bounds.
Balaam says he warned he could speak only God's word. Forewarned. He kept God's bounds.
Balaam says he warned he could speak only God's word. Forewarned. He kept God's bounds.
'There shall come a Star out of Jacob, and a Sceptre.' The Star. He foretells the coming King.
'There shall come a Star out of Jacob, and a Sceptre.' The Star. He foretells the coming King.
'There shall come a Star out of Jacob, and a Sceptre.' The Star. He foretells the coming King.
Edom shall be a possession; Israel shall do valiantly. Dominion. The kingdom shall prevail.
Edom shall be a possession; Israel shall do valiantly. Dominion. The kingdom shall prevail.
He looks on Amalek and the Kenites, foretelling their end. Nations. Their doom is named.
He looks on Amalek and the Kenites, foretelling their end. Nations. Their doom is named.
He looks on Amalek and the Kenites, foretelling their end. Nations. Their doom is named.
He foretells ships from Chittim; then departs. Departed. The prophet goes his way.
He foretells ships from Chittim; then departs. Departed. The prophet goes his way.
He foretells ships from Chittim; then departs. Departed. The prophet goes his way.
